evidence.scientific_clarity: Six-cluster KDE ridgeline with mild overlap matches brief exactly; cluster ordering and distribution shifts are clearly readable.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans — the matplotlib default — text is not selectable or editable.
evidence.palette: Green (C2) + red (C3) adjacent pair fails deuteranopia; colors are not perceptually sequential despite the comment claiming so; red implies directionality.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout with rect=[0.12,0,1,0.97] and overlap=0.6 uses space well; left margin for labels is adequate; no overlapping text observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure and states a directional finding; reviewer may challenge 'progressive upregulation' claim without statistical support or ordering rationale.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains Type 3 (bitmap) fonts only; script never sets rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is not selectable and will rasterize at print.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (32 KB), figure.png (62 KB), caption.tex (483 B), gen_figure.py (2.4 KB) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also saved.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption specifies KDE method, Scott's bandwidth, n≈400–500 per cluster, names specific clusters (1, 5, 6) with directional claim of progressive upregulation.

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial; the ridgeline layout faithfully fulfills the brief's 6-cluster KDE stacking requirement.
Caption is specific and informative: it names the statistical method, sample sizes, and makes a concrete directional finding about cluster expression levels.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0): the PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap fonts (DejaVu Sans default), making text non-selectable and unsuitable for journal submission.
Palette is neither colorblind-safe nor truly sequential: the red–green C2/C3 pair fails deuteranopia, and the color order does not encode the claimed progression from low to high expression.
